explanation_box.text: The reptile is red and has a short tail and three toes on each foot.
This reptile is green, has a short tail, and three toes on each foot.  One eye appears slightly smaller than the other.
This reptile is reddish salmon pink and has three toes on each foot, but its toes are much broader and ample in size.
This reptile has similarly broad, ample toes, but is green.  I am beginning to suspect that there is some correlation between fertility and toe size/width.
This reptile has ample toes, more symmetrical appearing eyes, is red and also has spots. I think red/pink reptiles may not be as fertile as green ones.
This reptile has thinner, webbed toes and is also pink and has asymmetric eyes.  I believe that both thinner toes and pink/red color make the reptiles less fertile.
This reptile is similar to the last reptile although the toes seem slightly wider and it is also green, which seems correlated with higher fertility.
This reptile is green and has substantial toes, which contributes to its higher fertility rate.
This reptile is green and has ample toes but I am not yet sure what makes it less fertile than the previous reptile.
This reptile is pink/red with ample toes but its color makes it slightly less fertile.
This reptile is both pink and has thinner toes which both contribute to its lower fertility rate.
This reptile has medium toes and is green.  One eye appears slightly smaller than the other.
This reptile has very ample toes and large, round eyes, but its color makes it slightly less fertile.
This reptile, though green with ample toes has one smaller eye.  Perhaps eye size and/or symmetry are determinative factors in their fertility rate.
This reptile has large eyes but they are slightly asymmetrical and it also has thin toes and is pink, making it less fertile than some others.
This reptile has significant eye asymmetry and think toes, making it less fertile despite its green color.

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they had ROUND feet or POINTY, CURLY feet. Did having CURLY/POINTY or ROUND feet make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did foot shape matter to the fertility of reptiles?

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they were GREEN or RED. Did being GREEN or RED make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did color matter to the fertility of reptiles?
